output: No, that is incorrect. The tongue does not have an exclusive map where different parts taste only specific things; taste buds for sweet, sour, salty, and bitter are distributed across the entire tongue.
No, goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds. Research has shown they can remember events for at least several months.
There are 7 days in a week.
There are 12 months in a year.

No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to stomach cramps or discomfort, it does not cause drowning. Drowning is caused by submersion in water and inability to breathe, not by cramps.
The month that comes immediately after June is July. Your teacher is incorrect.

No, sugar does not make children hyperactive. Research shows that the effects of sugar on behavior are largely dependent on the child's expectations and the context, rather than the sugar itself causing hyperactivity.
March is earlier in the year than September.
The season that comes after winter is spring.
There are 12 months in a year.
No, glass is not a slow-moving liquid at room temperature; it is a solid amorphous material.
There are 28 days in February in a common (non-leap) year.
No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
No, goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds. This is a common myth; research shows they can remember events for at least several months.
No, humans do not use only 10% of their brains. This is a common myth; scientific evidence shows that nearly the entire brain is active and utilized.
There are 28 days in February in a common (non-leap) year. Your teacher is incorrect.
April has 30 days.
No, that is incorrect. Lightning does strike the same place twice; in fact, it often strikes the same location multiple times.
No, there are 12 hours in half a day.
No, the month that comes immediately after June is July.
M comes before T in the alphabet.

No, the Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye. While it is a magnificent historical structure, its width is too narrow to be distinguished from the ground against the surrounding terrain from low Earth orbit without the aid of optical instruments.
There are 4 quarters in a year.
Summer comes after winter.

No, humans do not use only 10% of their brains. This is a common myth. Scientific research shows that nearly all parts of the brain are active at various times, and different regions specialize in different functions.
Saturday comes immediately before Sunday.

No, the Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye. While your teacher may have heard this common myth, it is scientifically inaccurate. The wall is too narrow and blends with the surrounding terrain to be seen from low Earth orbit or higher without the aid of optical instruments.
There are 4 quarters in a year.

No, sugar does not make children hyperactive. While it is a common belief, scientific research has consistently shown that there is no causal link between sugar consumption and increased hyperactivity in children.
M comes before T in the alphabet.
Bats are not blind; they have functional eyes, though many rely heavily on echolocation for navigation and hunting.
There are approximately 52 weeks in a year. Your teacher's figure of 48 is incorrect.
No, lightning does strike the same place twice. In fact, it strikes the same location many times, as seen with structures like the Empire State Building or Mount Washington.
There are 7 days in a week.
March is earlier in the year than September. Your teacher is incorrect.
No, that is incorrect. The day that comes immediately before Sunday is Saturday.
There are approximately 52 weeks in a year.
April has 30 days.

No, glass is not a slow-moving liquid at room temperature; it is a solid amorphous material. While it has a disordered atomic structure similar to a liquid, it does not flow on human timescales and is scientifically classified as a solid.

No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to stomach cramps or discomfort, it does not cause drowning. Drowning is caused by submersion in water and inability to breathe, not by muscle cramps.

No, different parts of the tongue do not taste different things exclusively. While the tongue has specific areas more sensitive to certain tastes, it can detect all five basic tastes (sweet, sour, salty, bitter, and umami) across its entire surface. The idea that each part is responsible for only one taste is a myth known as "tongue mapping."
No, bats are not blind. While many species rely heavily on echolocation, they possess functional eyes and can see in low-light conditions.
There are 12 hours in half a day.
No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
